verdict: PASS — all 5 acceptance criteria verified with positive and negative evidence. Root cause addressed. No regression. Single line modified as specified.

qa.overall_assessment: commands/commit.md line 157 grep predicate correctly changed from bare task-id grep to machine-readable HTML comment marker grep. All acceptance criteria pass with hard evidence. Fail-closed branches at lines 161 and 162 are structurally unchanged. Exactly one line modified.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Section header still reads '(3) Mtime + literal-task-id glob (final stage).' — this is stale relative to the new marker-grep predicate. BA spec explicitly restricted changes to line 157 only. A follow-on ticket should update line 156 to 'Mtime + marker-grep (final stage).' for consistency.

Stage 3 now requires producer (spec-continuation workflow) to emit '<!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> -->' in spec files for the marker to be found. No producer-side change landed in this cycle (explicitly out of scope per BA Won't Have). Stage 3 will produce an empty candidate set for existing specs until a future producer cycle adds marker injection to the spec-creation workflow.

checks.name: Prose-only reference is not a candidate
checks.evidence: grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->' docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md returned exit 1 (no matches). Both files contain prose references to 20260519-211515 but no marker. The new predicate correctly excludes them.

checks.name: Marker-bearing spec IS a candidate
checks.evidence: Positive fixture test: created a temp file containing '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->' and ran grep -lF against it. Result: exit 0, temp file path printed. The new predicate correctly identifies a marker-bearing spec as a candidate.

checks.name: Fail-closed multi-candidate policy preserved (line 162)
checks.evidence: grep -nF 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage' commands/commit.md matched at line 162 exactly. Text and exit-non-zero policy unchanged from prior state.
checks.command: grep -nF 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage' commands/commit.md
checks.result: 162:    - If set has multiple elements: print `multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage: <paths>; explicit context.spec_path required` and exit non-zero (fail-closed).

checks.name: Fail-closed single-candidate policy preserved (line 161)
checks.evidence: grep -nF 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' commands/commit.md matched at line 161. Text and exit-non-zero policy unchanged from prior state.
checks.command: grep -nF 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' commands/commit.md
checks.result: 161:    - If set has exactly one element: print `spec produced this cycle but not linked in context: <path>` and exit non-zero (fail-closed).

checks.name: Pre-existing false-positive specs gracefully excluded
checks.evidence: grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->' docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md returned exit 1. Both files that caused the original false-positive incident contain only prose references and are correctly excluded.
checks.result: exit 1 — zero matches (same as AC1, stable result)

checks.name: Prose description updated to machine-readable marker grep
checks.evidence: grep -nF 'literal-task-id grep' commands/commit.md returned exit 1 (phrase absent). grep -n 'machine-readable marker grep' commands/commit.md returned 1 match at line 157. Both conditions satisfied.

provenance_check.diff_evidence: git diff --name-only 1a1d058 includes commands/commit.md. git diff 1a1d058 -- commands/commit.md shows exactly +1/-1 at line 157. No other lines in commands/commit.md modified. All other files in the baseline diff are unrelated to this task.
provenance_check.diff_excerpt:
-    Else glob `docs/dev/specs/spec-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.md` ... run `grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md` ... literal-task-id grep.
+    Else glob `docs/dev/specs/spec-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.md` ... run `grep -lF "<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->" candidate.md` ... machine-readable marker grep.

No blockers found by codex.

Findings 2 and 7 are OBSERVATION_ONLY — logged in out_of_scope_observations, no verdict impact.

observations: Line 156 section header 'Mtime + literal-task-id glob (final stage).' is stale relative to the new predicate — BA spec restricted changes to line 157 only. Follow-on ticket recommended.

Stage 3 will produce empty candidate sets for all existing task-ids until a future cycle adds marker-injection to the spec-creation producer workflow. This is the intended graceful degradation (BA AC4).
